title: Stand up dinfra shared Caddy edge and migrate dtasks behind it
description: Executed 2026-07-19, ahead of all dcash work per owner request. Created github.com/evgenii-prusov/dinfra (caddy compose + Caddyfile + .env template + README runbook), cloned to ~/dinfra on the VM. Created docker network web; pre-connected the running dtasks app container with alias dtasks-app; pre-seeded dinfra_caddy-data from dtasks_caddy-data so no cert re-issuance; cutover (stop old caddy, start dinfra caddy) took seconds. dtasks PR 71 merged: app-only compose joining web with alias, Caddyfile removed, deploy docs updated. VM reconciled (git pull + compose up -d --remove-orphans). Verified https://dtasks.dev before and after; CD run on the merge green. Old dtasks_caddy-* volumes kept as rollback until dcash is live and stable.

title: E9 Deploy dengi.dev: Docker image, shared Caddy edge, CD, backups
description: Dockerfile (multi-stage: npm build then uv Python image, linux/arm64) + docker-entrypoint.sh running alembic upgrade head; docker-compose.yml with app only, dcash-data volume, DCASH_DB_PATH/SESSION_DIR/SECURE_COOKIES/PUBLIC_URL fixed, .env for DCASH_INVITE_CODE + optional OAuth; app publishes no ports and joins external docker network web with alias dcash-app. The shared edge ALREADY EXISTS and serves dtasks.dev: repo evgenii-prusov/dinfra, cloned to ~/dinfra on the VM (migrated 2026-07-19). Remaining edge work at first deploy: uncomment the DCASH_DOMAIN block in dinfra Caddyfile, add DCASH_DOMAIN=dengi.dev to ~/dinfra/.env, git pull + docker compose up -d in ~/dinfra (requires the DNS task done first). CD workflow copied from dtasks cd.yml (buildx arm64, ghcr.io/evgenii-prusov/dcash, ssh compose pull/up); copy SERVER_HOST and SSH_PRIVATE_KEY secrets into the dcash repo. Port scripts/backup_db.py + daily cron on the VM. Ref: spec section 10.
acceptance_criteria: https://dengi.dev serves the app with a valid cert; dtasks still up on its domain; push to main auto-deploys; nightly backup file appears in the volume.

title: T1 Point dengi.dev DNS A record at the VM reserved IP (user action)
description: Owner action: in the dengi.dev registrar DNS, create an A record @ pointing to the VM reserved public IP (terraform output public_ip in dtasks/infra, or the OCI console). Needed before Caddy can pass the ACME challenge - .dev is HSTS-preloaded, HTTPS only. Blocks the first production deploy.

title: E3 Core ledger: accounts, two-level categories, transactions, transfers
description: Models + Alembic migrations for currencies (seed EUR/USD/RUB), accounts (types, opening balance, archive), category_groups + categories, transactions, transfers - all integer minor units, household-scoped. CRUD API + merged GET /api/ledger month stream. Views: Accounts cards with native balances; Transactions month ledger with filters, inline quick-add and edit; transfer flow incl. cross-currency two-amount entry (implied rate); Settings categories editor. Ref: spec sections 3, 5, 6.
acceptance_criteria: Can create accounts in 3 currencies, record income/expense/transfers, balances correct incl. negative card balances.

title: E2 Auth and households: port dtasks auth, add tenancy and join codes
description: Port dtasks auth wholesale (argon2, invite-gated signup, server-side FileStore sessions, rate limit, timing-safe login, optional Google/GitHub OAuth) per dtasks docs/auth.md. Add households, household_members (owner/member, one household per user), household_invites single-use 7-day join codes. Signup path A: global DCASH_INVITE_CODE creates a household; path B: join code joins an existing one. Seed default two-level category tree per new household. Welcome view port incl. join-code variant; route guard. Settings: members list, generate/revoke invites. Ref: spec sections 3 and 9.
acceptance_criteria: Two users can end up in one shared household via a join code; all API routes household-scoped.
close_reason: E2 auth & households fully implemented: argon2 auth, two-path signup (A=new household, B=join code), FileStore sessions, rate limit, OAuth (Google+GitHub), household CRUD with IDOR protection, frontend auth guard, WelcomeView with join-code mode, queryClient 401 handler, logout. 20 backend tests + 10 frontend tests all pass.

title: E1 Scaffolding: repo, backend+frontend skeletons, design tokens, CI
description: Mirror dtasks layout (backend/, frontend/, Makefile, pre-commit). Backend: Litestar app factory, async SQLAlchemy, Alembic wired, DCASH_ env config, ruff+pytest. Frontend: Vite + React 19 + TS, TanStack Router/Query, Tailwind 4 with tokens from docs/design-system.md, @fontsource fonts (Plus Jakarta Sans, Lora, Inter), i18next EN/RU, theme toggle, app shell with sidebar. CI workflow (lint+test both sides). Create GitHub repo evgenii-prusov/dcash (public), branch protection, port dtasks .beads-hooks, fill CLAUDE.md build commands. Ref: docs/spec.md sections 2 and 11.
acceptance_criteria: make start serves the empty shell app locally with theme+language toggles working; CI green on GitHub.

title: T7: split editor + grouped ledger rendering
description: TransactionsView.tsx: a 'split' action on TxRow beside delete (:154), shown only for type=='transaction' with split_group_id === null, toggling an inline panel. Line 1 pre-filled with the current category and full amount; '+ Add line' appends a row pre-filled with the REMAINING amount so the common case needs no arithmetic. Live 'Remaining' readout tinted --warn while non-zero; Save disabled unless remaining is exactly 0 with >=2 lines. Plus grouped rendering of split rows in the ledger, and a split badge on DashboardView.tsx:76 recentLedger.
design: GROUPING: fold entries before mapping (~:484) - walking in order, a transaction with an unseen non-null split_group_id opens a group node collecting ALL entries with that id. Group BY ID, NEVER BY ADJACENCY: the sort key is (date, created_at) and correctness must not depend on siblings landing together. Header shows payee (falling back to account name), date, summed amount, child count, chevron, and a delete wired to useDeleteSplitGroup behind the existing two-step confirm; collapsed by default. EXCEPTION: render children FLAT with a split badge whenever a filter can return a PARTIAL group, since a header summing part of one would lie - that is filterCategoryId (matches one sibling) AND q (siblings share a payee but carry their own notes). The account filter is safe: siblings share account_id so the whole group always comes back. Owns TransactionsView.tsx lines 55-160 and the entries map (~484) - disjoint from T8's region.
acceptance_criteria: Splitting 60.00 into 38.00/12.50/9.50 leaves the account balance UNCHANGED and makes Dashboard spending-by-category show all three. Filtering by one split category renders that row flat with a badge, no misleading header. Deleting the group from its header restores the pre-split balance.
notes: Done on feat/split-editor; merged into epic

title: T5: i18n keys for splits + omnibox (en.json + ru.json)
description: Add all keys for BOTH features at once under 'ledger': omniboxPlaceholder, omniboxHint (the sigil legend), detailed, createCategoryIn, recentMerchants, split, splitTitle, addLine, remaining, splitMustMatchTotal, splitNeedsTwoLines, splitBadge, deleteSplit, and problems.* for the preview messages.
design: Landed as one task up front precisely so T7 and T8 never collide in en.json/ru.json. The RU date keywords the parser accepts (segodnya, vchera, month prefixes) live in the PARSER, not in i18n - it must accept both languages regardless of the active UI locale.
acceptance_criteria: i18n.test.ts passes - both files stay key-identical.
notes: PR #13 open; merged locally into epic/transaction-splits

title: T4: frontend API layer (types, client, hooks)
description: types.ts: split_group_id on Transaction; new TransactionSplitLine, TransactionSplitPayload, TransactionSplitCreate, PayeeSuggestion. client.ts: splitTransaction(id, payload), createSplitTransaction(payload), deleteSplitGroup(groupId), listPayees() beside createTransaction (:86) - api.createCategory ALREADY EXISTS at client.ts:80, reuse it. hooks.ts: useSplitTransaction, useCreateSplitTransaction, useDeleteSplitGroup, usePayees, useCreateCategory.
design: All API contracts are frozen in the approved plan, so this does not wait on T1/T2. Split/create/delete hooks invalidate ['ledger'], ['accounts'], ['reports'] exactly like useCreateTransaction (hooks.ts:152), PLUS ['payees'] so a new merchant is suggestible immediately. usePayees uses key ['payees'] with a generous staleTime - it is a filter-in-memory list, not a per-keystroke query. useCreateCategory invalidates ['categories'] and REPLACES the ad-hoc inline mutation at SettingsView.tsx:39 so there is one definition.
acceptance_criteria: npm run lint and npm test green; SettingsView still creates categories through the shared hook.

title: T3: omnibox parser (lib/omnibox.ts) - CRITICAL PATH
description: Pure function parseOmnibox(input, ctx) -> ParsedEntry. No React. Grammar: '!' amount (!60, !60.50, !60,50), '#' account (matched only, never created inline), '@' category (autocompletes, can create), bare date token (21.07, 21/07, 21.07.26, '21 jul', today/segodnya, yesterday/vchera), '//' note swallows the rest, leftover words = merchant. ParsedEntry carries amountMinor, account (resolved + raw query), lines[{category, categoryQuery, amountMinor}], date, payee, note, problems[]. Riskiest, highest-effort piece; zero dependencies - start it first.
design: Split at '//' first -> head + note. Scan head left to right; a sigil opens a field consuming words UP TO THE NEXT SIGIL so multi-word names work (@Public transport). Date-shaped tokens lifted out wherever they sit, once - '!' owning the amount is what makes a bare 21.07 unambiguously a date. Words before the first sigil = merchant. LINE ASSOCIATION: each @category claims the !amount that follows it; an !amount before any @ is the TOTAL - with one category it is the amount, with several it must equal their sum and a mismatch is a problem, not a silent fix. BUG TO AVOID: normalize ','->'.' before parseFloat - parseFloat('12,40') is 12, so the existing Math.round(parseFloat(x)*100) idiom must NOT be reused verbatim; RU users type commas by default. UNRESOLVED != ABSENT: absent @category or #account -> default (today / last-used account from localStorage / first non-archived); PRESENT BUT UNMATCHED -> a blocking problem for both sigils, because a typo'd #cahs falling back to the default account silently corrupts balances, and defaulting a category to Other silently corrupts reports.
acceptance_criteria: lib/omnibox.test.ts covers: each sigil alone; all combined; multi-word names; both decimal separators; every date form in EN and RU; inline-split forms with and without a leading total; the total-vs-sum mismatch; unresolved account and category names. npm test green.
notes: PR #12 open; merged locally into epic/transaction-splits

title: T1: split model + endpoints (migration, models, schemas, transactions.py)
description: Backend foundation for transaction splits. Migration 0005_transaction_splits.py adds nullable transactions.split_group_id + ix_transactions_split_group_id (plain add_column; SQLite is fine for a nullable column, no batch_alter_table). models.py Transaction gains split_group_id. schemas.py gains TransactionSplitLine/TransactionSplitPayload/TransactionSplitCreate and split_group_id on TransactionOut + LedgerTransaction. transactions.py gains a shared validator plus POST /{id}/split, POST /splits, DELETE /splits/{group_id}. ledger.py passes split_group_id through (~line 110). POST /api/transactions stays unchanged.
design: Shared validator _validated_split_kind(session, hh, lines, expected_total) -> str enforces: >=2 lines; every amount_minor > 0; sum(lines) == expected_total (exact - integer minor units, spec 3, never floats); every category resolves in the household AND all share one CategoryGroup.kind. POST /{id}/split REUSES the original row as line 1 (set category/amount/note + split_group_id = its own id) rather than delete-and-recreate, preserving id/created_at/created_by/payee; rejects rows that already have split_group_id (v1 boundary). All work happens in ONE session.commit() - N creates + a delete driven from the frontend could leave a half-split ledger and a wrong balance. Literal 'splits' path segment cannot collide with /{tx_id:int}.
acceptance_criteria: Split of 60.00 into 3 lines returns 3 rows sharing one split_group_id summing to 6000, and the account balance from GET /api/reports/summary is UNCHANGED. GET /api/reports/categories attributes amounts to all 3 categories. Rejects: sum mismatch, zero/negative line, single line, categories of differing kind, re-splitting a split row, another household's tx (404). POST /splits creates a group in one call. DELETE /splits/{id} removes all rows and restores the pre-split balance. ruff check . + ruff format --check . + pytest all green.
notes: PR #11 open; merged locally into epic/transaction-splits

title: Transaction splits + omnibox quick-add
description: Split one transaction across several categories (sibling rows sharing a nullable split_group_id), and replace the structured quick-add row with a single omnibox field using sigils: '!' amount, '#' account, '@' category, bare token = date, '//' note, leftover words = merchant. Merchant autocompletes from household payee history. Full design: docs plan 'Omnibox quick-add + splitting a transaction across categories'.
design: Splits are N sibling transactions rows sharing split_group_id (= id of the first row), NOT a parent row or a child splits table. Rationale: every aggregation (reports.py balances + category rollup + net worth, accounts.py, future budgets E6) sums the transactions table uniformly; sibling rows keep that invariant untouched so reports become correct for free. split_group_id is presentation metadata only - no aggregation reads it. NOTE: sigils deliberately invert the dtasks convention ('#' is account here, '@' is category). Do not 'fix' this. v1 is split-once: no re-editing a saved split; correction = delete the group and re-enter.
notes: All 9 tasks complete and merged into epic/transaction-splits. Branch is 20 commits ahead of origin/main, all quality gates green (79 backend tests, 49 frontend tests, ruff check+format clean, tsc clean). NEXT SESSION TODO: (1) git push -u origin epic/transaction-splits; (2) gh pr create --title 'feat: transaction splits + omnibox quick-add' targeting main; (3) merge with Rebase and Merge strategy on GitHub; (4) after merge: bd close dcash-97g dcash-97g.1 dcash-97g.2 dcash-97g.3 dcash-97g.4 dcash-97g.5 dcash-97g.6 dcash-97g.7 dcash-97g.8 dcash-97g.9; (5) close stale PRs #9-#13 with 'superseded by epic PR'.

title: E8 ZenMoney import: one-time CSV importer with mapping and rate backfill
description: Wizard: upload ZenMoney CSV export, dry-run preview (row count, date range, accounts/currencies, categories, unparsable rows), mapping step (ZenMoney account to DCash account with create-on-import; ZenMoney category to group/subcategory), commit as one import_batch; batch list + delete = undo. Parser: semicolon CSV with paired income/outcome columns; rows with both sides filled become transfers (both amounts preserved). Verify exact column set against a real export from the owner BEFORE building. Trigger historical rate backfill for the imported range. Ref: spec section 7.
acceptance_criteria: Full ZenMoney history lands with correct accounts, categories, transfers and EUR-converted historical reports; deleting the batch removes exactly its rows.

title: E5 Dashboard and reports: month summary, category breakdown, net worth trend
description: Endpoints: /api/reports/summary (income, expenses, net, per-account balances native+EUR), /api/reports/categories (group to subcategory rollup, kind filter), /api/reports/net-worth (monthly points at month-end rates). Dashboard view: EUR cards, budget status strip (once E6 lands), spending-by-category chart, net worth line, recent entries. recharts with colors from design tokens. Load the dataviz skill before writing any chart code. Ref: spec sections 5 and 6.
acceptance_criteria: Dashboard shows correct EUR-converted month summary and charts for a multi-currency household.

title: E4 FX rates: frankfurter + CBR fetchers, storage, overrides, EUR conversion
description: rates table (date, currency, rate_to_eur TEXT, source auto/manual). Provider interface + two httpx fetchers: frankfurter.app (ECB - USD and most currencies, no RUB after 2022-03) and CBR XML_daily/XML_dynamic (RUB). Lazy fetch on first authenticated request of the day + POST /api/rates/refresh; on-demand historical backfill; nearest-previous-day lookup; manual override wins. Decimal conversion service (half-even to minor units), never persist converted values. EUR equivalents shown on accounts and ledger. Settings rates UI with override. Ref: spec section 4.
acceptance_criteria: Daily EUR/USD/RUB rates appear automatically; historical range backfill works; an override changes past reports retroactively.

title: Add category move (group reassignment) in settings
description: When a category is created via the omnibox quick-add in the wrong parent group, there is no way to move it. The backend CategoryPatch schema lacks a group_id field, and the settings UI only offers archive/unarchive. Need: (1) add group_id to CategoryPatch + patch_category handler, (2) add inline edit UI per category in SettingsView with name + group dropdown.

title: T9: update docs/spec.md for splits + omnibox
description: spec.md is the source of truth and this touches it. Section 3: add split_group_id to the transactions row with a one-line rationale. Section 5: add the four new endpoints - POST /api/transactions/{id}/split, POST /api/transactions/splits, DELETE /api/transactions/splits/{id}, GET /api/transactions/payees. Section 6 Transactions view: document the omnibox sigil grammar and the split flow.
design: No code dependency - can land any time. Note in section 13 that receipt ATTACHMENTS remain out of scope; the receipt is the user's reason for splitting, not data we store.
acceptance_criteria: Sections 3, 5 and 6 describe splits and the omnibox accurately enough that the feature could be rebuilt from the spec alone.

title: T8: OmniboxAdd component + quick-add panel swap
description: components/OmniboxAdd.tsx - the input, its dropdown and the live preview chip row, plus swapping it into the TransactionsView quick-add panel (~450-473). Dropdown contents depend on which run the caret is in: '@...' -> CategoryPicker, '#...' -> accounts (name + currency), the LEADING BARE-WORD run -> merchants from usePayees(). Preview chip row straight from parseOmnibox with unresolved parts tinted --warn; Add disabled while any problem is present. '(gear) detailed' toggle swaps in the existing structured fields PRE-FILLED from the current parse. QuickAddTransfer and the transfer button are untouched.
design: Merchant suggestions filtered in memory case-insensitively, prefix matches ranked above substring, then the endpoint's usage/recency order - so 'ed' surfaces EDEKA first. Dropdown suppressed once the run is an exact match so it never blocks typing a brand new merchant; unmatched merchant text is ALWAYS accepted (unlike #/@, a merchant is free text, not a foreign key). Selecting a merchant with a top_category_id pre-fills @category ONLY when no @ has been typed yet - visible in the preview for confirmation, never silent, and any typed @ overrides it. Submit routing: 1 line -> api.createTransaction; >=2 lines -> api.createSplitTransaction. Owns the quick-add panel region ~450-473 - disjoint from T7's region in the same file; whichever rebases second should expect a trivial merge.
acceptance_criteria: 'EDEKA !60 #cash @groceries' previews as 60.00 / Groceries / Cash / today / EDEKA and creates one row. 'REWE #cash @groceries !38 @self-care !12.50 @home !9.50 yesterday // weekly shop' creates a 60.00 group dated yesterday with the note. 'LIDL !12,40 #cash @groceries 19.07' parses the comma decimal and the back-date. Typing 'ed' offers EDEKA from history.
notes: Done on feat/omnibox-component; merged into epic

title: T6: shared CategoryPicker component
description: components/CategoryPicker.tsx - the '@' dropdown used by BOTH the split editor and the omnibox. Shows matches as name + muted group name; when nothing matches, the last option is 'Create X in >' expanding to the household's groups, which calls useCreateCategory() and selects the new id. Optional kindFilter constrains offered groups to one kind. Also de-dups the ad-hoc mutation at SettingsView.tsx:39.
design: Port the interaction model from ../dtasks/frontend/src/components/QuickAddTask.tsx:34-80,169-224 - caret query regex, showAutocomplete/selectedIndex state, up/down/Enter/Tab/Escape handling, click-outside via dropdownRef, dropdown markup (absolute top-full ... border-line bg-surface). This is the only way the app creates a category outside Settings.
acceptance_criteria: Creating a category inline puts it in the chosen group and it appears under that group in Settings > Categories. npm run lint + npm test green.
notes: Done (salvaged after session-limit failure); merged into epic

title: T2: payees endpoint (merchant history for autocomplete)
description: GET /api/transactions/payees -> list[PayeeSuggestion(name, count, last_used, top_category_id)]. One grouped query scoped by household: SELECT payee, COUNT(*), MAX(date) FROM transactions WHERE household_id = ? AND payee IS NOT NULL AND payee != '' GROUP BY payee ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC, MAX(date) DESC. top_category_id is the payee's most-used category_id from the same scan. Cap at 500 rows. Needed because the ledger query only covers one month, so the client cannot derive merchant history itself.
design: Returns the whole list so the client filters in memory - no request per keystroke. A household's distinct merchants number in the hundreds at most. Depends on T1 for file hygiene only (~30 lines, shares transactions.py and schemas.py); logically independent.
acceptance_criteria: Ranks a twice-used merchant above a once-used one, reports the right top_category_id, excludes NULL/empty payees, and NEVER leaks another household's merchants.
notes: Done on feat/payees-endpoint; merged locally into epic/transaction-splits. No PR yet (stacked on epic).

title: C1 Wire DoltHub sync for dcash beads
description: Mirror the dtasks beads sync setup: create a DoltHub repository for the dcash database under the evgenii account, set sync.remote in .beads/config.yaml, run bd dolt push from this machine (DoltHub credentials already present - dtasks pushes to evgenii/dolt01). Add the remote-agent sync caveats to CLAUDE.md same as dtasks documents them.
notes: Resolved with beads default sync instead of DoltHub: bd dolt remote add origin git@github.com:evgenii-prusov/dcash.git + bd dolt push - Dolt data lives in refs/dolt/data on the dcash git remote (the beads-recommended default; dtasks-style DoltHub can be added later if ever needed).

title: E7 Recurring transactions: rules and lazy materialization
description: recurring_rules model: expense/income/transfer templates (account(s), category, amounts, payee, note), freq monthly/weekly/yearly with day spec, next_run, active. Lazy materializer: cheap once-per-day-per-household check on any authenticated request + POST /api/recurring/run; created rows link recurring_rule_id. Recurring view: rules list with next dates, pause/resume, run now. No scheduler container. Ref: spec sections 3, 5, 6.
acceptance_criteria: A monthly rent rule auto-creates its expense on first visit after the due date; pausing stops it.

title: E6 Budgets: monthly per-category limits with progress
description: budgets table (category_id, month YYYY-MM, amount_minor, always EUR). API: GET per month with limit+spent+remaining, PUT upsert, POST copy-previous. Budgets view month grid: progress bars, over/under coloring with warn tokens; spending converted at transaction-date rates; dashboard budget strip. Ref: spec sections 3, 5, 6.
acceptance_criteria: Set a monthly limit per subcategory, see progress against multi-currency spending, copy budgets to next month.

title: Trim account types: remove checking, deposit, other
description: When creating an account the UI shows 6 types (checking, savings, cash, card, deposit, other). This is too many for a personal household tracker. Remove the three types that have no clear use case: checking (same as savings for most users), deposit (niche), and other (a catch-all that obscures data). Keep: savings, cash, card. Changes needed: backend/app/schemas.py ACCOUNT_TYPES frozenset, the comment in backend/app/models.py, any Alembic migration or CHECK constraint if one exists, and the frontend account-creation form.
close_reason: Removed checking, deposit, other; kept savings, cash, card across backend schemas, model comment, frontend types, AccountsView, and tests.
